description.task: Model a task dependency system using a directed acyclic graph (DAG). Implement Kahn's algorithm (BFS-based topological sort) to determine a valid execution order of tasks.
instructions: Step 2 inside while: for(int nb:adj.get(curr)){if(--inDegree[nb]==0) queue.add(nb);}
If order.size() != tasks, a cycle exists and an empty list is returned
One valid topological order for the given deps: [0, 1, 2, 3, 4] or [0, 2, 1, 3, 4]
The cyclic graph should return an empty list
description.hint: for(int i=0;i<tasks;i++) if(inDegree[i]==0) queue.add(i); Then in while: for(int nb:adj.get(curr)) if(--inDegree[nb]==0) queue.add(nb);

description.task: Model a social network as an undirected graph where nodes are users and edges are friendships. Implement methods to find the degree of separation (shortest friendship path) between two users using BFS.
instructions: BFS in degreesOfSeparation: while(!queue.isEmpty()){String cur=queue.poll(); for(String nb:graph.get(cur)){if(!dist.containsKey(nb)){dist.put(nb,dist.get(cur)+1); if(nb.equals(to)) return dist.get(nb); queue.add(nb);}}}
mutualFriends: get friends of a into a Set, then filter friends of b who are in that set
Alice→Dave: Alice-Bob-Carol-Dave (3) or Alice-Eve-Carol-Dave (3), so answer is 3
Mutual friends of Alice and Carol: both know Eve and Bob — check intersections
description.hint: mutualFriends: Set<String> fa=new HashSet<>(graph.get(a)); for(String f:graph.get(b)) if(fa.contains(f)) result.add(f);

description.task: Implement a bipartite graph checker that determines whether a graph can be 2-colored (useful for conflict-free scheduling, e.g., exam timetabling). Use BFS coloring: try to color the graph with 2 colors; if a conflict is found, the graph is not bipartite.
instructions: BFS from start: color[start]=0; queue=new LinkedList<>(); queue.add(start);
While queue not empty: u=queue.poll(); for each nb in adj[u]: if color[nb]==-1, set color[nb]=1-color[u] and enqueue; else if color[nb]==color[u] return false;
The outer loop handles disconnected graphs
Square (0-1-2-3-0) is bipartite: {0,2} get color 0, {1,3} get color 1
Triangle (0-1-2-0) is an odd cycle — cannot be 2-colored → returns false
description.hint: color[start]=0; Queue<Integer> q=new LinkedList<>(); q.add(start); while(!q.isEmpty()){int u=q.poll(); for(int nb:adj[u]){if(color[nb]==-1){color[nb]=1-color[u];q.add(nb);}else if(color[nb]==color[u]) return false;}}
description.expectedOutput:
Square is bipartite: true
Triangle is bipartite: false
